files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 6fee48a..4ca3ba4 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -75,2 +75,9 @@ Day 70 / Week 10 observations:
 - Competitor posts showed two quality boosts around day 69/70, raising the risk that current expectations drift faster than low global dev can offset.
 - Week 10 adjustment: hold prices and capacity, shift a little S1 referral spend into S2 content, raise S2-targeted support, and increase global/S2 dev to defend quality without starting unaffordable R&D.
+
+Day 77 / Week 11 observations:
+- Cash fell to $146k because subscription payments ($124k) did not cover the $170k+ weekly operating base after the support/dev increase.
+- S2 content marketing still works: 612 paid content subscribers from 2,237 leads, with 207 B and 233 C users. S1 referral collapsed to only 3 paid subscribers from 186 leads, so S1 paid acquisition should stop.
+- Cancellations rose to 31, mostly low-ARPU S1 A users, while S2 cancellations stayed modest. S2 remains the economic center.
+- Open issues remain same-day, and resolved issues averaged 1 day; support complaints persist socially, but the spend level can be trimmed while retaining S2 coverage.
+- Week 11 adjustment: stop S1 ads, keep S2 content, reduce global/S1 ops burn, keep meaningful S2 support/dev, raise B/C list prices modestly, and publish concrete support throughput metrics.
